steps.title: Turn the years into points
steps.content: The input $t$ is years after 2000. So 2000 gives the point $(0,4)$, and 2013 gives the point $(13,1.9)$, where the output is measured in millions of barrels.

steps.title: Fit the line in Desmos
steps.content: Enter the two points and run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $b=4$ and $m=-0.161538\ldots$, which is $-\frac{21}{130}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The model is $f(t)=-\frac{21}{130}t+4$. Choice C has the correct negative rate and the correct initial value.
